goldenComments: permDenialCache caches denials for arbitrary user-controlled resource names without a size limit, enabling memory exhaustion DoS.

findings.label: security
findings.oneSentenceSummary: Unbounded denial cache on user-controlled keys
findings.suggestionContent: WHAT: permDenialCache stores denials for arbitrary resource names with no size bound. WHY: An attacker can spam Check with random names and OOM the process. HOW: Use an LRU with a fixed max entries.

findings.label: bug
findings.oneSentenceSummary: Cache key concatenates name and parent with underscores
findings.suggestionContent: WHAT: userPermDenialCacheKey joins name and parent with underscores without escaping. WHY: name=foo_ parent=bar collides with name=foo parent=_bar. HOW: Use a delimiter that cannot appear or encode the parts.
